ijakt6i9kovspdsjvi2sldonkk.summary: You must navigate critical shifts across your technology portfolio—the DNS Transformation cadence has transitioned to active execution, and security mandates have successfully pivoted the two-way SMS pilot toward secure channel escalation. Resource bottlenecks currently threaten parallel Celonis initiatives—driven by Dan Griffiths' high-priority mandate—while the Claims Evolution team prepares for potential fifty percent funding cuts. To stabilize ongoing delivery, you must address procurement flow ambiguities and integrate the paused Intelligent Document Processing questionnaire work with parallel efforts.
tasks: Resolve the Celonis resource bottlenecks—prioritizing Dan Griffiths' ninety-day process mining mandate over lower-priority visualization pilots.
Pivot the SMS proof of concept scope—focusing strictly on secure asynchronous chat escalation to satisfy the CISO's security guardrails.
Finalize the updated BPO notebook architecture diagram—completing the unfinished draft left on the Wiki before the review deadline.
Secure Total Cost of Ownership validation from Michael Summers—ensuring compliance with the post-tech procurement flow for Category 1 items.
Merge the paused IDP questionnaire analytics into Phil Stackhouse's parallel project—utilizing Kamali Kampati's work as an immediate delivery accelerator.
ijakt6i9kovspdsjvi2sldonkk.title: Weekly Staff – P&C CTO Leadership

9r4o3jhghc0h17o2hh6thnnndc.summary: You face significant resource bottlenecks across three parallel Celonis initiatives—the Pay Claim Visualization, the BPO Notebook scorecard pilot, and Dan Griffiths' high-priority 90-day mandate. To mitigate this contention, the team is delaying broader process mining tool decisions—focusing current task mining RFP efforts on specific vendor seat pricing—until the Celonis pilot wraps up. You must ground your observability strategy in the established enterprise definitions—clearly separating offline process mining, visual dashboards, and near-real-time monitoring.
tasks: Resolve the resource bottlenecks delaying the Pay Claim Visualization and BPO Notebook pilot.
Prioritize Dan Griffiths' 90-day Claims Process Mining Mandate to ensure its immediate execution.
Request tiered pricing proposals from the selected task mining vendors including Stereo Logic and Celonis.
Align the Observability Data Strategy with the established process mining and monitoring definitions.
9r4o3jhghc0h17o2hh6thnnndc.title: Observability Data Strategy meeting

5ujo3470tapdo2b8jh4c3chpno.summary: Review your June 29 session with Mike—focusing on critical resource shortages and major model failures across your P&C initiatives. Address the team headcount reduction—down to 14 or 15 people despite a doubled workload—and provide succession plans for key roles. Prepare updates for the locked application rationalization list to target the fifty-million-dollar hygiene fund—while resolving the unusable property loss model and the category three control test failure.
tasks: Deliver succession plans for yourself and James—addressing the explicit requests from leadership.
Update the locked application rationalization spreadsheet—targeting the fifty-million-dollar hygiene fund before McCarty's baseline solidifies.
Resolve the unusable property loss model—highlighting the lack of a P&C data architect and the push to bring back Chris.
Address the category three control test failure—establishing a clear remediation path for the MII issue.
5ujo3470tapdo2b8jh4c3chpno.title: Mid-Year Check-In w/Mike Wilkins | Mark Francis
